audit_result.proof_trace: Selector assumes descending order and returns first match via find_map. Named network constructors (sepolia L512-520, holesky L637-645, hoodi L739-747) define blob_schedule in ascending order, bypassing serde sorting. With ascending data, first match returns lowest epoch instead of highest.
audit_result.attack_scenario: On Sepolia at epoch 280000, selector returns entry (274176, max_blobs=15) instead of correct entry (275712, max_blobs=21). Node rejects valid blocks with 16-21 blobs, causing consensus failure.

PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-029.text: get_blob_parameters sorted schedule must select the highest-epoch entry not exceeding the query epoch.
PROP-56ad1eb2-inv-029.assertion: forall epoch e: get_blob_parameters(e) == entry with max(entry.EPOCH) where entry.EPOCH <= e
